milestone: Cloud Sync — real red/yellow/green traffic-light signal
slice: Direct, screenshot-driven request: the top-bar #wb-cloud-sync badge's dot was a mixed set of emoji glyphs (☁️/🌤️/⚠️/⚪) that don't render consistently per OS/font and don't map cleanly onto a real traffic-light meaning at a glance -- "yellow for sending/syncing data with cloud, green all synced, red something wrong happened." _setCloudSyncState() (worldBuilderApp.js) now toggles one of four new .wb-signal-dot-{green,yellow,red,grey} CSS classes on a plain coloured circle instead of writing an emoji glyph: pending/backing-up -> yellow (a soft pulsing animation, skipped under prefers-reduced-motion, so "actively working" reads as motion, not just colour); synced/"Backed up" -> green; error and conflict ("Cloud has newer changes") -> red, since both genuinely need the creator's attention; "unavailable" (Supabase not configured in this deployment at all) stays a deliberate fourth, neutral grey rather than being folded into red or green -- "not configured" is a different fact from "configured and something is wrong," disclosed rather than glossed over. index.html's #wb-cloud-sync-dot span dropped its hardcoded ☁️ glyph in favour of the new .wb-signal-dot class; every existing state's own text label/title/click-to-force-overwrite behaviour (the conflict escape hatch) is completely unchanged -- only the dot's own rendering changed. The local-save badge (#wb-workspace-saved, 🟢/🟠/🔴 for dirty/saved/error) is untouched, since the request specifically named cloud sync ("sending/syncing data with cloud"), a genuinely separate indicator from the local write. Verified via a new scratch Playwright check confirming all four signal classes render as real 9px circles with the correct RGB colours, that yellow alone carries the pulse animation while the other three stay static, and that no emoji text remains in the dot element; both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder architecture change; confined to tools/world-builder-v2/index.html, css/world-builder.css, and js/worldBuilderApp.js.
